title: Lace conformance test vector
description: A single conformance test case, distributed in lacelang-testkit and consumed by every executor's CLI under test. Discriminated by `type`. The testkit reads vectors from disk, invokes the executor's `parse` / `validate` / `run` subcommand per the executor manifest, normalises the actual output (stripping fields listed in `expected.ignore`), and diffs against `expected`.

id.description: Stable identifier within its directory. Used in test reports.

description.description: One-sentence summary of what the test proves. Surfaced in failure reports.

spec_refs.description: Optional spec section references (e.g. 'lace-spec.md §4.1'). Documentation only.

requires.description: Feature areas this vector exercises. When the testkit is run with `--omit` (or a manifest declares `[conformance].omit`), any vector whose `requires` intersects the omit set is skipped with reason 'omitted: <feature>'. Vectors without a `requires` list are always runnable. See lace-spec.md §16.

input.description: Inputs vary by `type`. See the `oneOf` discriminator.

ParseExpected.description: Either a successful AST or a list of parse errors.

ast.description: Expected AST template. Must conform to ast.schema.json in practice, but typed as generic object here so `ignore` paths can contain placeholders. Actual AST emitted by the executor's `parse` subcommand is validated against the full ast schema at test time.

errors.description: Expected parse errors. Order-insensitive; testkit treats this as a multiset match.

variables.description: Names of declared $vars. Values are not needed for validation.

extensions.description: Extension names assumed active for this validation. The testkit configures the executor accordingly.

errors.description: Expected validation errors. Order-insensitive multiset match.

ValidationContext.description: Validator-facing system limits from lace.config. Does not include recurrence — Lace has no recurrence context; wait-ceiling is a platform-level check (spec §12).

variables.description: Flat variable map injected as $var resolutions.

prev_results.description: Previous run result injected via --prev-results. Typed as generic object here so vectors can supply minimal prev payloads with just the fields the test's expressions reference.

http_mock.description: Indexed by call_index. Tells the testkit's bundled HTTP server how to respond. Test server does TLS too — see also tls_scenario.

tls_scenario.description: Cert scenario for HTTPS calls. Defaults to `valid`. Other values use bundled bad certs.

body.description: Response body bytes (UTF-8 string in the vector for portability).

delay_ms.description: Server-side delay before sending response. Lets executor measure realistic timings.

ttfb_delay_ms.description: Delay before first byte (controls TTFB independently of total).

redirect_to.description: If set, server returns 30x with Location: this URL.

prev_results.description: Previous run result injected via --prev-results. Typed as generic object here so vectors can supply minimal prev payloads with just the fields the test's expressions reference.

content.description: Inline TOML body of the .laceext file. The testkit writes it to a temp file before invoking the executor.

result.description: Expected ProbeResult template. Intentionally typed as generic object rather than a strict ProbeResult — any path listed in `ignore` is stripped from the actual result before comparison, so the expected may contain placeholder values at those paths. The actual result emitted by the executor is validated against the full ProbeResult schema at test time.

IgnorePaths.description: JSON-pointer-like paths to strip from the actual output before diffing. Supports `[*]` for array wildcards (e.g. 'calls[*].started_at'). Default ignores: 'started_at', 'ended_at', 'calls[*].started_at', 'calls[*].ended_at', 'calls[*].response.body_path' — the testkit applies these unconditionally; this field adds to them.

code.description: Error code from the canonical registry (error-codes.json). Validators must emit these exact codes.

callIndex.description: Zero-based call index where the error occurred. Optional; omit when the error is script-level.

chainMethod.description: Which chain method the error is on, if applicable.

field.description: Specific field name within the chain method.

line.description: Source line. Optional — testkit will not assert this if omitted.
